data.title: M-tron awarded over $12M production contract from major supplier to Boeing, Airbus
data.description:
• M-tron (NYSE:MPTI) on Wednesday announced a multi-year agreement valued at more than $12 million from a top supplier to the avionics and aerospace industries including Boeing and Airbus.
  • The contract includes over 45 different products, including high-performance radio frequency filters, crystal resonators, TCXOs, OCXOs, and precision clock oscillators.
  • MPTI is +2.06% to $47.37.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Carnival Corporation poised to snap six sessions of losses
data.description:
Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) shares were on track to snap six straight sessions of losses on Wednesday, as it gained 2.17% to $19.81 during afternoon trading.

The cruise operator lost nearly 9% in the preceding six session. The stock closed in the red at $19.39 on Tuesday.

In the year so far, the company has declined nearly 22%, compared to a 4% loss in the broader S&P500 index. However, in the past year, Carnival Corporation’s shares have gained over 23%.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, the company has a Strong Buy rating with a score of 4.65 out of 5 lifted by an A in profitability and a B- rating in growth and momentum.

Seeking Alpha analysts had a Buy recommendation for the firm’s shares and the Wall Street community echoed the sentiment with a similar rating. Overall, 22 analysts gave the company a Buy and above rating while 6 recommended to Hold the stock while 2 rated it as a Strong Sell. 

Last month the company delivered its first quarter earnings where it posted a non-GAAP EPS of $0.13 beating estimates by $0.11 and a revenue of $5.81B surpassing analysts’ projections by $70M.

However, its shares were dragged down by its FQ2 EPS guidance which it pegged at $0.22, double from a year ago, but a penny below expectations.

Seeking Alpha analyst Mehul Singh believes Carnival Corporation stands to further progress in its post-COVID rebound benefitting from the bankruptcy of smaller players. Further, Carnival’s competitive pricing and the cruise industry’s high barriers to entry, positions it well for growth, he argued.

data.title: Marsh McLennan Agency acquires Arthur Hall Insurance
data.description:
• Marsh McLennan Agency, a business of Marsh (NYSE:MMC) announced the acquisition of Arthur Hall Insurance, a West Chester, Pennsylvania-based full-service insurance agency.
  • Terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DraftKings shares rise after six consecutive sessions of losses
data.description:
DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG) shares snap six straight sessions of losses, as the stock was up 3.1% at $34.22 on Wednesday.

The digital sports entertainment and gaming company lost nearly 15% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has lost nearly 10% so far this year, while in the year 2024, DKNG climbed nearly 11%, compared to a 24% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

DKNG is down 17% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, DKNG has a Buy rating with a score of 3.6 out of 5. The company received D in the prospect of profitability, C in momentum, while it got a A growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, out of 33 analysts, 21 gave DKNG a strong Buy rating. Seven analysts have given the stock a Buy recommendation, while five recommended Hold. None see the stock as a Sell. 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

"The excellent and accelerating growth momentum will likely continue in 2025 as DKNG's customer base swells," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: Non-store and on-line shopping to fuel $5.42 trillion in 2025 retail sales - NRF
data.description:
The National Retail Foundation expects retail sales to reach $5.42 trillion this year, an increase of 2.7% to 3.7% from last year, thanks to continued momentum in the economy and led by strength in non-store and online shopping.

“While we do expect slower growth, consumer fundamentals remain intact, supported by low unemployment, slower but steady income growth, and solid household finances. Consumer spending is not unraveling,” said NRF Chief Economist Jack Kleinhenz.

While the group cautioned that uncertainty surrounding U.S. trade policy is weighing on consumer and business confidence, the NRF does not believe this will result in an immediate drop in consumer spending.

With the implementation of tariffs, the NRF expects the personal consumption expenditure price index (“PCE”) to remain unchanged at the 2.5% level. Additionally, household balance sheets remain healthy, credit card and loan delinquencies have risen but remain in line with the pre-pandemic trend, and the consumer credit outlook should remain healthy as long as the labor market remains solid.

Related tickers: Consumer Staples Select SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLP), Consumer Discretionary Select SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLY).

data.title: CRISPR Therapeutics set to snap six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP) shares were on track to snap six straight sessions of losses on Wednesday, as the stock rose over 2% to $34.03 in afternoon trade.

The Switzerland-based biotech company lost about 19% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has lost more than 13% in the last one year, compared to the over 4% fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

CRSP is down 21.5% over the past one month. The stock closed 2.6% lower on Tuesday at $33.14.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, CRSP has a Hold rating with a score of 2.75 out of 5. The score was dragged down as the stock received C for momentum and valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 17 analysts gave CRSP a Buy and above. 10 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and one recommended Strong Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

“Upcoming milestones and updates on CRISPR Therapeutics is currently sitting at historical support, at a valuation that is clinical assets are expected to drive shareholder value back to previous highs,” argued a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

The stock, in February, was upgraded by Evercore on upcoming catalysts, with the brokerage saying that it was now including the CTX320 and CTX310 in vivo programs in its price target, with probabilities of success of 10% and 5%, respectively.

data.title: BlackBerry shares record losses for seven consecutive sessions; disappointing outlook weighs
data.description:
BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) shares fell for seven straight sessions, as the stock was down 9.5% at $3.38 on Wednesday.

The technology company issued an outlook for fiscal 2026 that fell short of Wall Street's estimates. BlackBerry's forecast for the upcoming quarter also fell short of expectations, as it expects sales to be between $107M and $115M, below the $128.4M analysts were anticipating. 

The weaker-than-anticipated guidance comes on the back of better-than-expected fourth-quarter results, which were driven in part by BlackBerry's three segments, QNX, Secure Communications and Licensing

BB shares lost over 14% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has slipped over 12% so far this year, while in the year 2024, BB gained over 10%, compared to the 24% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

BB is down 23% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, BB has a Buy rating with a score of 4.1 out of 5. The company received D+ in the prospect of profitability, and A in momentum and growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, one analyst gave BB a Strong Buy rating. Five analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Strong Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

 

"BB stock's valuation is fair compared to peers, but consistent revenue declines could provide an overhang on shares in the near term," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: BAE Systems gets $70 million contract for production of missile tubes
data.description:
• BAE Systems (OTCPK:BAESY) said it has secured a  $70 million contract award for the production of Virginia Payload Module missile tubes for Block VI Virginia-class submarines.
  • The contract is from General Dynamics Electric Boat.
  • "These missile tubes deliver critical firepower to the Virginia-class submarine fleet, a cornerstone to U.S. national security," said Charles Lewis, director of submarine programs for platforms & services.

data.title: Macy's to claw back $600K in exec bonuses tied to accounting error - Bloomberg
data.description:
• Macy’s (NYSE:M) is taking back more than $600,000 in executive bonuses that resulted from an overstated earnings metric that inflated compensation, according to Bloomberg citing a filing by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • The earnings metric, which was used to calculate executive bonuses, was overstated by ~$81M in 2023 resulting in $609,613 in overpaid bonuses, of which $257,520 has already been retrieved.
  • The error was identified during an internal accounting audit last year, which revealed $154M in delivery costs that were intentionally concealed by an employee.
  • Due to the erroneous exclusion of these costs, Macy’s (NYSE:M) was able to show increased profits due to lower delivery costs. The discovery of the cost manipulation forced Macy’s (NYSE:M) to delay its third quarter results and restate its profit outlook.

data.title: TJX Companies is seen as a retail standout amid concerns about tariffs and the economy
data.description:
Jefferies sees TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX) as well-positioned for growth in the off-price retail sector.

Analyst Corey Tarlowe noted that in an environment of surplus inventory that poses markdown risks for many retailers, TJX (NYSE:TJX) could stand out by capitalizing on the conditions due to its strong operational model and market positioning.

CEO Ernie Herrman has emphasized the availability of inventory in the coming months, which presents significant buying opportunities for TJX's experienced team of over 1,300 buyers. The strategic approach is expected to enhance margins further, with FY25 gross margin reaching a peak of 30.6%, up 60 basis points year over year. Jefferies views TJX's FY26 gross margin as conservative, given the prior outperformance.

Also working in TJX's (TJX) favor, channel checks by Jefferies revealed robust in-store product availability across categories like luxury apparel, home goods, and seasonal items, reinforcing the retailer's ability to meet diverse consumer demands while driving growth and profitability.

The long-term outlook for TJX (TJX) also looks favorable to Jefferies.

"We believe TJX should benefit from the secular migration toward the Off-Price sector, which, in our view, will likely lead to share gains from other, more traditional retailers. Additionally, we believe Home and International expansion represents unique growth opportunities for TJX," highlighted Tarlowe.

Jefferies has a Buy rating on TJX (TJX) and a price target of $150 (+20% upside potential).
